title: Tests fail when running in a single CPU environment
author: jamessan
body:
Some of the Debian QA processes run mass rebuilds on small VMs, including a single CPU VM. That found some neovim-qt tests fail in such an environment, mainly tst_shell.

```
FAIL!  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(set *) Compared values are not the same
   Actual   (cmd_clip.takeFirst().at(2)): QVariant(QByteArray)
   Expected (QVariant(register_data))   : QVariant(QByteArray,something)
   Loc: [./test/tst_shell.cpp(270)]
QWARN  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(set *) QProcess: Destroyed while process ("nvim") is still running.
QWARN  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(empty +) QProcess: Destroyed while process ("nvim") is still running.
PASS   : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(empty +)
FAIL!  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(set +) Compared values are not the same
   Actual   (cmd_clip.takeFirst().at(2)): QVariant(QByteArray)
   Expected (QVariant(register_data))   : QVariant(QByteArray,something)
   Loc: [./test/tst_shell.cpp(270)]
QWARN  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(set +) QProcess: Destroyed while process ("nvim") is still running.
FAIL!  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(paste *) Compared values are not the same
   Actual   (cmd_clip.takeFirst().at(2)): QVariant(QByteArray)
   Expected (QVariant(register_data))   : QVariant(QByteArray,something)
   Loc: [./test/tst_shell.cpp(270)]
QWARN  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(paste *) QProcess: Destroyed while process ("nvim") is still running.
FAIL!  : NeovimQt::TestShell::GetClipboard(paste +) Compared values are not the same
   Actual   (cmd_clip.takeFirst().at(2)): QVariant(QByteArray)
   Expected (QVariant(register_data))   : QVariant(QByteArray,something)
   Loc: [./test/tst_shell.cpp(270)]
```

The easiest way to reproduce this is to use taskset to force everything to run on 1 core.

```
taskset --cpu-list 0 ctest ...
```

title: Tracking for Release 0.2.20
author: jgehrig
body:
There hasn't been a release in some time, and the Qt6 migration and Qt5 deprecation is worthy of a release.

We should create a pre-release soon, and aim to release a new version in the next few months.

Items Blocking Release
- [ ] Cleanup CI Pipeline

title: Feature request: macOS Option key as Meta/Alt toggle (GuiMacOptionIsMeta)
author: kgfly
body:
**Problem**
On macOS, pressing Option+key produces Unicode characters (e.g., Option+j → ∆, Option+b → ∫) instead of triggering <M-key> / <A-key> Neovim mappings. This makes all Meta key mappings unusable in neovim-qt on macOS.

Every other Neovim GUI and terminal emulator provides a toggle for this:

Neovide: vim.g.neovide_input_macos_option_key_is_meta = "both"
iTerm2: Profiles → Keys → "Option key acts as Meta"
Kitty: macos_option_as_alt yes
Alacritty: window.option_as_alt: Both
neovim-qt has no equivalent.

**Root cause**
This is not a Qt limitation. Qt provides all the necessary information in QKeyEvent:

Field	Option+j value	Available?
key()	Qt::Key_J	✅
modifiers()	Qt::AltModifier	✅
text()	∆ (macOS Unicode)	✅
The current input handler in src/gui/input.cpp (convertKey) uses text() which contains the macOS-transformed Unicode character. The raw key identity and Alt modifier are available but unused in this scenario.

Proposed solution
Add a GuiMacOptionIsMeta command (configurable from ginit.vim / ginit.lua) with values none (default, current behavior), left, right, or both.

When enabled, modify convertKey() in src/gui/input.cpp to:

```
// Pseudocode for the proposed change in convertKey():
#ifdef Q_OS_MAC
if (macOptionIsMeta != MacOptionMeta::None) {
    bool isLeftAlt  = ev.nativeModifiers() & NativeLeftAltMask;
    bool isRightAlt = ev.nativeModifiers() & NativeRightAltMask;
 
    bool treatAsMeta =
        (macOptionIsMeta == MacOptionMeta::Both) ||
        (macOptionIsMeta == MacOptionMeta::Left  && isLeftAlt) ||
        (macOptionIsMeta == MacOptionMeta::Right && isRightAlt);
 
    if (treatAsMeta && (ev.modifiers() & Qt::AltModifier)) {
        // Use key() to get the base key name, ignore text() Unicode transformation
        QString keyName = QKeySequence(ev.key()).toString().toLower();
        return "<A-" + keyName + ">";
    }
}
#endif
```

**Key points:**
Guarded behind #ifdef Q_OS_MAC — no impact on other platforms

Default none preserves current behavior — fully backward compatible
Coexists with the existing PR #841 fix (German/Spanish keyboard AltModifier stripping), which only applies when this toggle is off
nativeModifiers() can distinguish left vs right Option keys on macOS (left Option = 0x20, right Option = 0x40)
Example user config
lua
-- ginit.lua
if vim.g.GuiLoaded then
    vim.cmd('GuiMacOptionIsMeta both')  -- or "left", "right", "none"
end
Reproduction
lua
-- init.lua
vim.keymap.set('n', '<M-j>', function() print("Alt-J works") end)
Press Option+j in neovim-qt on macOS → inserts ∆ instead of printing "Alt-J works".

**Environment**
macOS (Apple Silicon / Intel)
Any keyboard layout where users want <M-key> mappings
neovim-qt latest

title: `vim.pack.add` fails on installing
author: mikew
body:
Having this error come up whenever I try `vim.pack.add`. No idea what could be doing it, works fine in `nvim` and Neovide.

```
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'confirm'
	...Cellar/neovim/0.12.1/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/pack.lua:575: in function 'confirm_install'
	...Cellar/neovim/0.12.1/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/pack.lua:715: in function 'install_list'
	...Cellar/neovim/0.12.1/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/pack.lua:920: in function 'lock_sync'
	...Cellar/neovim/0.12.1/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/pack.lua:957: in function 'lock_read'
	...Cellar/neovim/0.12.1/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/pack.lua:998: in function 'add'

```

```
$ nvim-qt --version

Build type: Release

  args: --cmd set termguicolors

Run "nvim -V1 -v" for more info
```

title: CI Improvement Tracking Issue
author: jgehrig
body:
Let's file an issue to track and discuss the CI changes we'd like to make. The area has gone quite stale and needs some work.

## Approved Tasks
- [ ] Add `build-test-win.yml` to `build-test.yml` so it is logically grouped in the UI. See [comment](https://github.com/equalsraf/neovim-qt/pull/1155/changes#r3086657704).
- [ ] Add intentional MacOS  `gcc` coverage. See [comment](https://github.com/equalsraf/neovim-qt/pull/1155/changes#r3086657120).
- [ ] Use GitHub Actions as primary CI. Deprecate AppVeyor + Azure.
    - [ ] Deprecate AppVeyor
    - [ ] Deprecate Azure Pipelines

## Tasks Requiring investigation:
- [ ] Investigate AppImage support. See #1079 
- [ ] Investigate Snap Package support. See #1132
- [ ] Investigate binary artifacts for nvim-qt on all platfroms. #1132
    - [ ] Win32
    - [ ] MacOS
    - [ ] Linux
- [ ] Windows should skip WinDeployQt when builds fail. See [build logs](https://github.com/equalsraf/neovim-qt/actions/runs/24565103384/job/71853475559?pr=1155).

title: Support neovim `:connect`
author: mikew
body:
Neovim 0.12 added a new `:connect` command which lets you connect to an existing neovim server. When running `:connect ...` in Neovim Qt the GUI hangs and the app doesn't close / needs to be killed.

Here's all the info I can really find on how to implement it so far: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/5035#issuecomment-3910753357

title: Deprecate Neovim API0 + API1
author: jgehrig
body:
It looks like the required version of LUA JIT for Neovim API0/AP1 longer builds on modern systems.

We should deprecate calls to APIs 0/1 and bump the minimum required Neovim version.

Here are some details:
> Trying to build neovim 0.1.5/0.1.7 from source - fails because old LuaJIT can't parse modern LuaRocks manifest (too large). These are ~10-year-old source trees fundamentally incompatible with modern systems.

title: Make Qt6 the minimum supported Qt version
author: twelveeighty
body: This issue is to track the efforts to raise the minimum supported Qt version to Qt6.

title: Line disappears when editing buffer
author: vanaigr

title: Add support for custom axes on variable fonts
author: iteratee
body:
Currently there is no way to modify a custom axis on a variable font. For example the [Recursive Font](https://www.recursive.design/) has the additional axes: MONO, CASL, and CRSV. A possible workaround is to use a font tool to name a particular instantiation. It would be nice to try them out in the editor before committing to reduce the feedback loop.

Ideally when using this font, the same `:<attr>` syntax could be used:

`GuiFont Recursive:h12:w550:slnt0:MONO1:CASL0.15:CRSV0.5`

Qt 6.7+ has support for variable fonts.

See PR #1150 for a suggested solution.

title: Linux alt key mapping does not work on Mac's option key mapping.
author: kgfly
body:
Linux alt key mapping does not work on Mac's option key mapping.

May you please suggest if there is a workaround? Thanks.

(On other nvim gui, such as neovide, alt-key, which becomes "option key", works fine)

title: How do i paste system clipboard in command mode or in search mode?
author: lmna
body:
What does work in neovim-qt: go to insert mode, then paste clipboard with a mouse click (clipboard contents gets pasted).
What does NOT work in neovim-qt: go to command mode (':') or to search mode ('/'), then paste clipboard with a mouse click (nothing happens).
What does work in neovim in terminal: all scenarios listed above (clipboard contents gets pasted all the time).

What am i doing wrong? I tried to search for a workaround, but did not find anything helpful.

author: midrare
body: On Windows, the installer does not create a shortcut in the start menu.

title: [BUG] `:cq` not wonks correctly under `Windows`
author: zdm
body:
Under Windows when I execute `:cq` command - it shows a message - `Nvim exited with error code x` and asks me to repeat action. What is the sense of this message? I do not need to repeat anything.

This is not correct - nvim-qt should just exit with the same code.
So currently it is not possible to exit nvim-qt with error on Windows and this makes it unusable for many tasks, where this feature is required. For example in git, when nvim used as diff or merge tool, etc...
I found other similar issues and it is possible that this was fixed for Linux, but for Windows - not.
Is it possible to fix this finally?

title: Bug report: command mode mapping does not work very well for some case.
author: kgfly
body:
```
local opts = { noremap = true, silent = true }
vim.keymap.set({'c'}, '<c-v>', '<c-r>*', opts)
```

does not work very well.

Observed:
I yank a dummy string, eg "adsfadsfasdf"
In command mode, I press c-v, tring to paste. I see.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6b4625b-45fc-421d-a759-673eb06c5b5e)

The pasted string is actually there. But the issue is: **it is not visible.**

I press entry key. It prompted

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0dadea60-949c-4c6f-bc10-b6804b0e3527), which means it has been pasted, but it's invisible.

title: Feature request: make http link clickable by mouse, after pressing ctrl key.
author: kgfly
body:
For nvim, with modern terminal, such as Windows terminal on Windows OS, http link is clickable by mouse after pressing ctrl key, which is super handy.

Please have similar feature in the gui software.
